Tile filter system

Tile filter system

Time metrics

Additional improvements on the filtering – sum/Integrated intensity

Additional improvements on the filtering – improved median threshold=3500

Additional improvements on the filtering – mean threshold=4000

Additional improvements on the filtering – mean+std+entropy - PCA+K-means

Perform dimensionality reduction (PCA or UMAP) and clustering (KMeans) on the simplified features of all tiles (mean DAPI, std DAPI, entropy). The clusters of pure background tiles can be recognized as cluster 1

Additional improvements on the filtering – Gaussian mixture model (GMM)

A Gaussian mixture model (GMM) is a machine learning method used to determine the probability each data point belongs to a given cluster. The model is a soft clustering method used in unsupervised learning.

components = 3

Pick the one with the lowest mean as background. Now we can automatically rank the GMM components and select the background cluster based on the lowest tile mean.

Conclusions on the filtering

We can use mean intensity + Gaussian mixture model (GMM)

Segmentation time can be estimated as 95s for each 2500*2500 tile, and 500-2000 tiles per image

So 8h-30h per image

Segmentation test - filtering using Gaussian mixture model (GMM) on sample S120

Median+GMM

Mean+GMM

Segmentation test

We started a pilot run of segmentation on S120. But due to false parameter setting, we only got masks from the first row of tiles – now it’s fixed and it’s running (should last for 8 hours).
Need for parallelization

With Ziqi and Lina’s help, we now have this huge function for GammaCorrection, UnsharpMasking, Creates tiles out of the input image, Run the GMM filtering function and run MESMER segmentation.
